title: Hurricane Ian (Lee benchmark)
benchmark_focus: Southwest Florida benchmark for severe wind, storm-surge overlap, and large-loss adjustment disputes.
key_facts: The insured property sustained roof failure, broken openings, and prolonged interior water exposure during Ian.
Local storm-surge reporting complicated the allocation between wind-driven rain and flood-related damage.
The initial carrier estimate scoped limited repairs despite photographs showing broad exterior and interior damage.
notes: Useful benchmark for Lee County fact patterns where wind and surge narratives compete.
Strong scenario for testing evidence dedup when many news and government pages discuss the same landfall facts.
